title: Sweet Fluffy Australian Pork Bake
description: A moist, caramelized pork bake with a sugarfree sweetsavory glaze that becomes delightfully fluffy-perfect for mealprep lunches.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F) and lightly oil a large oven‑safe skillet or baking dish.
Pat the pork cubes dry with paper towels. In a bowl, combine salt, pepper, baking powder, smoked paprika, garlic powder, and minced ginger, then toss the pork with the seasoning until well coated.
Heat the olive oil in the skillet over medium‑high heat. Sear the pork pieces until browned on all sides, about 5–6 minutes. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
Add the diced onions to the same skillet and cook, stirring, until translucent and slightly caramelized, about 4 minutes.
Stir in coconut aminos and monk fruit sweetener, deglazing the pan with a splash of water if needed. Cook for 1 minute until the sauce begins to thicken.
Return the pork to the skillet, pour in the coconut milk, and bring to a gentle simmer. Ensure the liquid coats the pork.
Cover with a lid or foil and bake in the preheated oven for 45–50 minutes, or until the pork reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C) and is tender.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let the pork rest for 5 minutes. Then gently mash or shred the pork with a fork or blend, incorporating the juices to create a fluffy texture.
Divide the bake into four airtight containers for meal prep. Cool to room temperature before sealing. Store in the refrigerator for up to 4 days or freeze for longer storage.
To reheat, microwave on high for 2–3 minutes until heated through, or reheat in a preheated oven at 160°C (325°F) until warmed.
